title: Signal transmission cable assembly with ungrounded sheath containing electrically conductive particles
abstract: A data transmission cable assembly (110) includes an elongate first conductor, an elongate second conductor, and a sheath (118) at least partially axially surrounding the first and second conductors (112A, 112B). The sheath (118) contains a plurality of electrically conductive particles (122) interspersed within a matrix formed of an electrically insulative polymeric material (120). The conductive particles (122) may be formed of a metallic material or and inherently conductive polymer material. The plurality conductive particles (122) may be filaments that form a plurality of electrically interconnected networks. Each network is electrically isolated from every other network. Each network contains less than 125 filaments and/or has a length less than 13 millimeters. The bulk conductivity of the sheath (118) is substantially equal to the conductivity of the electrically insulative polymeric material (120). The data transmission cable assembly (110) does not include a terminal that is configured to connect the sheath (118) to an electrical ground.
claims: Claims WE CLAIM: 1. A data transmission cable assembly (110), comprising: an elongate first conductor; an elongate second conductor; and a sheath (118) at least partially axially surrounding the first and second conductors (112A, 112B), wherein said sheath (118) comprises a plurality of electrically conductive particles (122) interspersed within a matrix formed of an electrically insulative polymeric material (120) and wherein the bulk conductivity of the sheath (118) is substantially equal to the conductivity of the electrically insulative polymeric material (120). 2. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of conductive particles (122) are formed of a metallic material. 3. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 2, wherein the plurality of conductive particles (122) are in the form of filaments. 4. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 3, wherein the filaments are metallic filaments. 5. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 3, wherein the filaments are metallically plated fiber filaments. 6. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 3, wherein the filaments are carbon nanotube filaments. 7. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 3, wherein the filaments are substantially aligned with one another. 8. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 3, wherein the filaments form a plurality of electrically interconnected networks, wherein each network is electrically isolated from every other network, and wherein each network contains less than 125 filaments. 9. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 3, wherein the filaments form a plurality of electrically interconnected networks, wherein each network is electrically isolated from every other network, and wherein each network has a length of less than 13 millimeters. 10. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the plurality of conductive particles (122) are formed of an inherently conductive polymeric material (120). 11. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the sheath (118) is formed via an extrusion process. 12. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the sheath (118) is in the form of a film (226) wrapped about the first and second conductors (112A, 112B). 13. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the first and second conductors (112A, 112B) are twisted one about the other. 14. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the first and second conductors (112A, 112B)are substantially parallel to one another. 15. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the assembly does not include a terminal configured to connect the sheath (118) to an electrical ground. 16. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the assembly comprises a plurality of first conductors (112A) and a plurality of second conductors (112B). 17. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, further comprising a metallic shield (328) at least partially axially surrounding the first and second conductors (112A, 112B), wherein the sheath (118) axially surrounds the metallic shield (328). 18. The data transmission cable assembly (110) according to claim 1, wherein the outer surface has a lower concentration of the electrically conductive particles (122) than an internal portion of the sheath (118).
